rationale: Both correctly land on the ground-truth disposition (duplicate of #4808/#4809), but X asserts as fact that the fix is 'already applied on main' and 'verified against current main checkout' plus an unverifiable detail about a test being 'removed' — overclaiming things it has no way to confirm from a rebase-branch checkout. Y reaches the same duplicate verdict but is explicit about what it could and couldn't verify (rebase branch vs. real upstream main, #4808's merge status), and adds a concrete, well-grounded follow-up (test coverage gap in test_kv_cache_scale_mapper.py) that makes it more useful and honest rather than confidently asserting unverifiable claims.
